<!-- Generated by purlin:spec-from-code. Review and refine. -->
# Anchor: schema_spec_format

> Scope: specs/**/*.md

## What it does

Defines the canonical 3-section format for all feature and anchor specs. Every spec file must contain `## What it does`, `## Rules`, and `## Proof` sections with correctly numbered identifiers. This anchor governs how specs are parsed by the MCP server and proof plugins.

## Rules

- RULE-1: Every spec MUST contain exactly three required sections: `## What it does`, `## Rules`, and `## Proof`
- RULE-2: Rules MUST be numbered sequentially as `RULE-1`, `RULE-2`, etc. — unnumbered lines under `## Rules` trigger a WARNING in sync_status
- RULE-3: Proofs MUST follow the format `PROOF-N (RULE-N): description` and each rule must have at least one proof
- RULE-4: Optional metadata `> Requires:` and `> Scope:` must appear as blockquote lines before the first section

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): A spec missing any of the three sections produces a WARNING or "no rules defined" in sync_status output
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): A spec with unnumbered lines under `## Rules` produces a WARNING with count of unnumbered lines
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): sync_status parses PROOF-N lines and maps them to RULE-N for coverage tracking
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): sync_status extracts Requires and Scope metadata from blockquote lines and uses them for dependency resolution and staleness detection
